title: AMC Entertainment Q1 2026 Results: Strong Recovery in Box Office
text:
AMC Entertainment reported substantial gains in its first quarter of 2026, with revenue climbing to over $1 billion, a significant jump from $862.5 million in the prior year period. This 20% increase reflects improving conditions in the theatrical exhibition market.

FINANCIAL PERFORMANCE
The company achieved adjusted EBITDA of $38.3 million, marking a dramatic turnaround from a negative $57.7 million the previous year. This represents the strongest quarter for the metric since the pandemic. CEO Adam Aron stated the results "are a clear testament to our disciplined operating execution" in maximizing revenue while controlling expenses.

Attendance rose 13% year-over-year, with the company operating approximately 9,300 screens. However, free cash flow remained negative at $175 million, though improved from negative $417 million.

OUTLOOK AND CONTRIBUTING FACTORS
The recovery stems from robust domestic box office performance alongside considerably improved international results, particularly across European operations. Box office revenues increased nearly 25% in the quarter.

Aron expressed confidence in continued growth, citing a strong upcoming slate including films like Project Hail Mary, The Super Mario Galaxy Movie, and The Devil Wears Prada 2. He also highlighted studios' commitment to maintaining a 45-day exclusive theatrical window, supporting the exhibition industry's competitive position.
